stem: For the polar curve \( r=[[a]]+[[b]]\cos\theta \), find \( \dfrac{dy}{dx} \) at \( \theta=\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[n]]} \).
solution: Using \( x=r\cos\theta \), \( y=r\sin\theta \), with \( r'(\theta)=-[[b]]\sin\theta \), the slope is \( \dfrac{dy}{dx}=\dfrac{r'\sin\theta+r\cos\theta}{r'\cos\theta-r\sin\theta} \), which at \( \theta=\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[n]]} \) evaluates to \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This treats the curve as if it were the unit circle, ignoring \( r(\theta)\) entirely.
This uses the reciprocal trig ratio instead of the full quotient-rule formula.
This uses the raw ratio \( a/b\) in place of correctly combining \( r\) and \( r'\) in the formula.
This drops the \( r'\sin\theta\) and \( r'\cos\theta\) terms from the numerator and denominator entirely.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct slope.
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct slope.

stem: For the rose curve \( r=[[a]]\cos([[p]]\theta) \), find \( \dfrac{dy}{dx} \) at \( \theta=\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[n]]} \).
solution: With \( r'(\theta)=-[[a]]\cdot[[p]]\sin([[p]]\theta) \) from the chain rule, the slope is \( \dfrac{dy}{dx}=\dfrac{r'\sin\theta+r\cos\theta}{r'\cos\theta-r\sin\theta} \), which at \( \theta=\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[n]]} \) evaluates to \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This treats the curve as if it were the unit circle, ignoring \( r(\theta)\) entirely.
This forgets that \( x=r\cos\theta,\,y=r\sin\theta\) mix \( r\theta \) and \( p\theta\) and instead differentiates as if \( r=a\cos\theta\).
This divides by the petal count \( p\) instead of correctly applying the chain rule inside \( r'\).
This forgets the chain-rule factor of \( p\) inside \( r'(\theta)\) but keeps it outside as a multiplier of the whole ratio.
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct slope.
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct slope.

stem: For the Archimedean spiral \( r=[[a]]\theta \), find \( \dfrac{dy}{dx} \) at \( \theta=\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[n]]} \).
solution: With \( r'(\theta)=[[a]] \), the slope is \( \dfrac{dy}{dx}=\dfrac{r'\sin\theta+r\cos\theta}{r'\cos\theta-r\sin\theta}=\dfrac{\sin\theta+\theta\cos\theta}{\cos\theta-\theta\sin\theta} \), which at \( \theta=\dfrac{[[k]]\pi}{[[n]]} \) evaluates to \( [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This treats the curve as if it were a circle of constant radius, ignoring the growing radius entirely.
This reports \( \theta\) itself rather than the slope of the tangent line.
This inverts the correct ratio, swapping numerator and denominator.
This forgets to divide by the denominator, reporting only the numerator's structure scaled by \( a\).
This is off by a small additive amount from the correct slope.
This is off by a small additive amount (other direction) from the correct slope.
